claim: The tip of the inner tube of the toxic type VI secretion system (T6SS) antibacterial effector in Escherichia coli (E. coli) carries toxic effector proteins.

result.claim: The tip of the inner tube of the toxic type VI secretion system (T6SS) antibacterial effector in Escherichia coli (E. coli) carries toxic effector proteins.
result.reasoning: The evidence strongly supports the claim that the tip of the inner tube of the toxic type VI secretion system (T6SS) antibacterial effector carries toxic effector proteins. Several propositions and chunks indicate this. Proposition 147 (Paper ID: 27731651) states that T6SS spike and tube components are propelled along with antibacterial and antieukaryotic effectors. Proposition 1907 (Paper ID: 12428814) explicitly mentions that effectors bind to the inner surface of Hcp, which is a component of the T6SS inner tube. Furthermore, propositions 1671 and 1672 (Paper ID: 4406819) highlight that PAAR proteins are involved in attaching effector domains to the VgrG spike complex, which sharpens the tip of the T6SS spike. Chunk 72 (Paper ID: 4406819) further reinforces this by describing a model where the VgrG-PAAR spike complex is decorated with multiple effectors. While some evidence refers to other bacterial species like *Pseudomonas aeruginosa* and *Vibrio cholerae*, the T6SS mechanism and its components are generally conserved across Gram-negative bacteria, making these findings relevant to *E. coli*.

evidence.text: CK666 treatment produces actin arcs in Drosophila S2 cells.
evidence.source: Arp2/3 complex inhibition radically alters lamellipodial actin architecture, suspended cell shape, and the cell spreading process

evidence.text: Autophagy is a recently recognized immune effector mechanism against intracellular pathogens.
evidence.source: T helper 2 cytokines inhibit autophagic control of intracellular Mycobacterium tuberculosis.

evidence.text: The bacterial type VI secretion system (T6SS) is an organelle that is structurally and mechanistically analogous to an intracellular membrane-attached contractile phage tail.
evidence.source: A view to a kill: the bacterial type VI secretion system.

evidence.text: The assay was applied by expressing mutated proteins as transgenes in cell lines.

evidence.text: PAAR proteins are further involved in attaching effector domains to the spike.

evidence.text: A rapid conformational change in the structure of a sheath protein complex propels T6SS spike and tube components along with antibacterial and antieukaryotic effectors out of predatory T6SS(+) cells and into prey cells.
evidence.source: A view to a kill: the bacterial type VI secretion system.
